units.content: physical measurement apparatus acquires at least one measurement record by interaction with a physical target
apparatus outputs measurement record to storage
storage holds a plurality of measurement records, each a directed entry with six fields: producing measurer id, target entity id, property id, numerical value, method id, source tag
source tag takes one of two values: first value indicates record produced by physical interaction; second value indicates record produced by computation
storage operates on append-only basis: subsequent record with same composite key overwrites prior record in storage while prior record is preserved in append-only event log external to storage
convergence analyser groups records by (target, property) pair
convergence analyser computes a relative-spread measure of numerical values for each group containing two or more records
convergence analyser classifies each group as converged, tension, divergent, or single according to relative-spread measure against predetermined thresholds
measurer-correlation analyser computes counts of each measurer's groups in each classification
measurer-correlation analyser classifies a measurer as systemic-crack source when two or more of its groups are divergent or tension
measurer-correlation analyser classifies a measurer as systemic-opening source when two or more of its groups are converged and none are divergent or tension
measurer-correlation analyser classifies a measurer as local-crack source when exactly one of its groups is divergent or tension

population-decomposition analyser determines, for a (target, property) group classified as tension or divergent, whether distribution of numerical values admits decomposition into two or more components under a Gaussian-mixture model selected by BIC improvement threshold, subject to minimum-component-size and minimum-inter-component-separation guards
when decomposition is found, analyser proposes split of target entity identifier into two or more sub-identifiers with each record assigned to exactly one sub-identifier
proposed split is effected on storage through rewriting of the target-entity identifier field of each record
feedback-signal generator emits a machine-readable feedback signal encoding the analyser's output for each measurer classification and each proposed identifier split

feedback-signal interface routes feedback signal to an actor configured to annotate the storage or to cause modification of how subsequent measurement records are acquired by the physical measurement apparatus
source tag is the sole basis for invoking the echo-chamber classifier and the pattern-requirement rule, gating downstream logic that distinguishes physical-interaction from computational evidence

physical-interaction records and computational records share the storage schema and are classified under the same grouping and relative-spread computations without reference to whether the producing measurer is a physical instrument or a computational measurer
source tag alone gates downstream logic distinguishing physical-interaction from computational evidence, because it is the sole distinguishing field on each directed entry
system does not comprise an ontology layer assigning semantic types to entities, properties, or methods
system does not comprise a separate object-type or property-type registry external to the storage
system does not comprise a separate model-card, model-registry, or model-configuration record external to the storage for characterising a producing measurer
any characterising attribute of a producing measurer held by the system is held as a directed entry in the storage under the same six-field schema as a measurement record of a physical target

all semantic labelling, if performed, is performed external to the claimed system and is not consumed by the convergence analyser, the measurer-correlation analyser, or the population-decomposition analyser

because the system excludes all external ontology, registry, and model-card structures, any measurer characterisation must reside in the same storage schema as measurement records, making the schema self-contained and uniform
